title: coronilla securigera
intro: Coronilla securigera, also known as the Squirrelcorn or Bird's Foot, is a perennial legume native to the Mediterranean region and parts of Asia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Coronilla securigera include its ability to lower fever, reduce pain, and act as an antibacterial agent, making it a popular herbal remedy for various ailments. The plant is often prepared as a tea, powder, or tincture, and is also used in traditional medicine to treat conditions such as arthritis and digestive issues.
